name: Web Lifecycle
description: Standard for a domain entity's lifecycle: a declared status set with guarded, role-scoped transitions and a canonical status presentation. A loan, a policy, a claim, an order, a ticket — each is a state machine (draft → submitted → … → closed) whose legal moves depend on the actor and on guard predicates. The transition map is declarative; whether an actor may fire a transition is delegated to a guard provider (Web Guards), and each transition is an observable event other standards (audit, reporting) compose on. Distinct from Web States (reactive state primitives) and from a region's lifecycle (Web Guards): this is the domain object's workflow state.
